overall_summary: The ElizaOS ecosystem is focused on technical optimizations and expansion of agent memory capabilities, while facing community pressure for official clarity on multi-chain token legitimacy. Significant plugin contributions for RAG-based persistence and deep-market analytics on Solana were introduced.
key_facts: Odilitime identified an unused or undocumented reply action optimization in the xfn-framework codebase for investigation.
ElizaOS confirmed native support for OpenAI-compatible API integration since its initial launch.
Meme Broker released the MEM0 plugin, a self-updating RAG system for persistent agent conversations.
The APEX Oracle v0.5.0 was launched to provide deep-market analytics and Sybil detection for Solana trading agents.
Token migration from ai16z to elizaos has officially ended and is no longer available to users.
A new community initiative was launched by satsbased to providing advisory support and amplification for Eliza tech projects.
ElizaBAO is developing a custom ElizaOS v2.0 integration for the Milady project featuring Polymarket plugins.
Official Solana contract address for ElizaOS was clarified as DuMbhu7mvQvqQHGcnikDgb4XegXJRyhUBfdU22uELiZA.
open_questions: If ElizaOS is spinning off tokens, which ones are legit?
Is there a way to wire in memU or mem0 or something similar?
How can I get started working with the project? (Unanswered due to scammer interference)

new_issues_prs.title: Major database refactor to improve long-term system architecture
new_issues_prs.author: unknown
new_issues_prs.significance: Foundational architectural change for system reliability and speed.

new_issues_prs.title: Integrated the SAID Protocol for on-chain Solana identity
new_issues_prs.author: unknown
new_issues_prs.significance: Enables verifiable agent signatures and digital passports on Solana.

overall_focus.claim: The project is in a high-velocity phase preparing for official beta launch, focusing on messaging integrations (WhatsApp, Gmail) and decentralized identity.

discord_updates.summary: Discussions centered on multi-chain token legitimacy (SOL vs BSC), OpenAI API compatibility, and the launch of a new builder support system.
Focus on memory integration solutions like memU and MEM0; new developers introduced full-stack and AI integration expertise.
Internal code audit discovery regarding potential reply action optimizations.

user_feedback.feedback_summary: Users expressed significant confusion regarding which ElizaOS tokens are official across Solana, BSC, and Base chains.
New users report persistent targeting by scam bots immediately after posting their first messages in discussion channels.

strategic_insights.insight: DorianD proposed a dual-sided marketplace model combining staking with inference compute credits to balance supply and demand.
implications_or_questions: Should ElizaOS adopt a 'freemium' staking model similar to Venice VVV?

strategic_insights.insight: The discovery of unused optimizations suggests the framework codebase may benefit from a formal cleanup prior to the v2.0 release.
implications_or_questions: Is there a process for auditing 'day one' features that may now be redundant?

market_analysis.observation: Venice VVV has demonstrated success with a 1 million user base and a tokenomics model that airdropped 50% of supply.
market_analysis.relevance: Serves as a benchmark for potential ElizaOS/Jeju tokenomics structures.

market_analysis.observation: Market liquidity and bridge UX are identified as high-priority factors for positioning in the upcoming bull market.
market_analysis.relevance: Directly impacts user onboarding and token stability across chains.
